title: 25 Piastres

obverse.description:
Purple and pale on pale lilac and multicolour underprint.
Al-Sayida Aisha Mosque at the centre.
obverse.lettering:
٢٥
قرشا
البنك المركزى المصرى
خمسَة وعِشرونَ قرشا

المحافظ
مسجد السيدة عائشة
٢٥ قرشا
obverse.lettering_translation:
25
Piastres [Qirsh]
Central Bank of Egypt
Twenty Five
Piastres

The governor
Al-Sayeda Aisha Mosque
25
Piastres

reverse.description: Egypt Coat of arms (The Eagle), corn, cotton, and wheat at the left-centre.
reverse.lettering:
Central Bank of Egypt
Twenty Five Piastres
25

comments:
Information :

  • This type was updated from  the previous design type, with lighter blue background - see links.
  • The letter prefix of this type is the same as the previous type, the letter  (ص) from Prefix 141 to 459 which is the last print of this denomination dated 28/12/2008.
  • The serial of each prefix has 7 digit number from 1 to 9,999,999 
  • To know the date of the notes from 1985 to 1999 you have to follow this example
                                                                                                   (Y DD MM Y) ٨٠٤٠٤٥ = 804045 = 04.04.1985
  • This type is considered the last issued banknote for the 25 piastres. 

 

Security Thread:

ST1.From prefix 141 to 164  (3.5.87 to  29.11.89), buried solid black S.T. with no writing on it - 

Picture is missing.

 

ST2.From prefix 163 to 219  (28.11.89 to 13.05.93), buried transparent S.T. written on it with black font “البنك المركزى المصرى ” (i.e. The Central Bank of Egypt) 

Picture is missing.

 

ST3.From prefix 220 to 286  (6.11.97 to 5.12.99), buried black S.T. engraved on it “البنك المركزى المصرى ” (i.e. The Central Bank of Egypt) 

Picture is missing.

 

ST4.From prefix 286 to 377  (5.12.99 to 24.9.06), buried black S.T. engraved on it  the denomination in Arabic “ ” (i.e. ٢٥ قرش) 

Picture is missing.

 

ST5.From prefix 377 to 459  (24.9.06 to 28.12.2008), buried black S.T. engraved on it  the denomination in Arabic “ ”  to be read in both sides of the note 

© Kiet Tran

 

Artistic Design Features 

  • Sayeda Aisha Mosque 

It is a mosque in Cairo, Egypt, that contains the tomb of a known female scholar Aisha bint Ja'far Al-Sadiq . It is located outside Citadel Square on a similarly named street. She was a pious and God-fearing worshipper. and lived in Egypt where she was greatly revered. She died in 145 AH (481 AD).

When Salahuddin al-Ayyubi ruled Egypt, he ordered the construction of a madrasah next to the shrine. The structure was restored and took its current form in 1762 AD by Prince AbdelRahman Katkhuda.

 

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sayeda_Aisha_Mosque

 

 

 

 

  • The Eagle Arab Republic of Egypt Coat of Arms

 

Since 1984 the Eagle was used as the coat of arms of Egypt and was used in the Egyptian flag instead of the  . falcon. This is one of the major differences between this type of banknote and its earlier type,  see links.

WM2: Tutankhamun (Small Size)
